```json
  "decision": "fail",

  "findings": [
    "Phase conductor identification tape colors do not match NEC required color coding for grounded/neutral or ungrounded conductors.",
    "Incorrect phase tape colors create risk of misidentification during terminations, testing, and future maintenance.",
    "Condition is a code violation and safety hazard before energization and inspection."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove or cover incorrect phase tape and reapply correct identification per NEC 200.6 and 210.5.",
    "Verify correct colors for each phase, neutral, and grounded conductor throughout the circuit.",
    "Re-inspect and document correction before closing out rough inspection."
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC 200.6 and 210.5 require specific identification of grounded conductors (white/gray) and ungrounded conductors by phase. Wrong phase tape colors at rough-complete stage make the installation non-compliant and create a high risk of miswiring, shock, or equipment damage. The defect is visible, measurable, and must be corrected before inspection or energization.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 200.6 Means of Identifying Grounded Conductors",
    "NEC 210.5 Identification for Branch Circuits"
  ]
}
```
